summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host info and filesystem data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 30) that pulls in child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/env/identity data, and POSTs to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds the behavior: it requires fs/os/https/child_process at the top level, calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, and issues https.request/https.get calls to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 142-161, 320). The combination of hardcoded C2 on a third-party static-hosting domain, system-identity collection (hostname, whoami, env), filesystem enumeration, and lifecycle-time + import-time triggers matches the credential/host-recon exfiltration fingerprint. The package name suggests a wallet-related utility, and the structural shape (system intel + filesystem walk + outbound POST to a non-publisher GitHub Pages site) is consistent with a wallet/credential stealer rather than any legitimate sentinel functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
